DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT GEORGE BENETATOS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant George Benetatos' demurrer to all causes of action alleged against him in the third amended cross-complaint filed by John Mungia and Trad'r Sam is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. The third amended cross-complaint fails to allege any explanation grounded in the evidence why the attorney-client civil conspiracy allegations in the second amended cross-complaint were omitted from the third amended cross-complaint. Nor does the third amended complaint provide any explanation for the alleging of the new allegations against Mr. Benetatos which are inconsistent with the allegations of the second amended cross-complaint. This court's ruling on the demurrer to the second amended cross-complaint did not relieve cross-complainants of the obligation to provide adequate explanations for the omission of the conspiracy allegations and the addition of allegations that are inconsistent with previously made allegations. The court concludes that as to the claims alleged against Mr. Benetatos the third amended cross-complaint is a sham pleading and an ill-disguised effort to avoid the requirements of Civil Code 1714.10(a). Because cross-complainants have not provided any evidence to show that they are able to allege adequate explanations for their omission of the conspiracy allegations and the addition of the new allegations, there is no basis for granting them leave to file a fourth amended cross-complaint. While the court admonishes Mr. Bentetatos for his failure to adhere to the meet and confer requirements for the filing of a demurrer, under the circumstances of this case the court exercises its discretion to decide the demurrer on its merits. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT GEORGE BENETATOS' Amended DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Benetatos's demurrer to the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") is sustained with twenty days leave to amend.

Despite cross-complaints' dismissal of causes of action two and fourteen for aiding and abetting underlying torts, the SACC still alleges claims that implicate Civil Code section 1714.10 [attorney-client conspiracy; court order]. (See also Cortese v. Sherwood (2018) 26 Cal.App.5th 445, 455 [the word "conspiracy" has no talismanic significance to implicate Civil Code section 1714.10]; Howard v. Superior Court (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 745, 749 [aiding and abetting allegations trigger application of Civil Code section 1714.20.)

In this case, the SACC continues to have numerous "conspiracy" allegations that are incorporated by reference in all of the claims. (See, e.g., SACC, pars. 25 [Benetatos counseled Riedel and Milon to violate an agreement and court orders]; 38 [Milon and Benetatos jointly concealed Riedel's incapacity]; 43-44 [Benetatos subordinated partnership interests in favor of Milon's; reference to "scheme"].) Cross-complainants have leave to delete the "conspiracy" allegations and/or clearly allege claims that fall under the exceptions to the pre-filing order requirement. (See Civil Code sec. 1714.10(c).)

The court has no opinion on whether the proposed Third Amended Complaint cures the cited defects.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Victor Hwang; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/VMH)
